title: Tavita's Touch
intro: Every piece begins as a shapeless lump of clay. What makes it beautiful is one thing — her hands. The brand is built on that single truth: these aren't just ceramics, they're the imprint of her touch.

subject.label: THE RAW MATERIAL
subject.text: It starts as nothing — a wet, shapeless lump. Every finished piece is just clay, plus her hands.

difference.label: THE IDEA
difference.text: The whole brand rests on one truth: the beauty isn't in the clay, it's in the touch. That's the name, and that's the strategy.

anchor.label: THE ANCHOR
anchor.text: Tavita is my wife. Watching her work, the idea — and the name that came with it — was obvious: she doesn't make objects, she leaves an imprint. The identity had to be that imprint too.

mark.label: THE MARK
mark.text: Two T's meeting like two hands closing around clay. One continuous line — the gesture of a touch, not a drawn letter.

typeColor.label: TYPE & COLOR
typeColor.text: A thin serif and a palette pulled straight from the clay. Restrained, exact — the mark of a hand in full control.

inWorld.label: IN THE MATERIAL
inWorld.text: On every piece, the mark is pressed into the clay — the last, most literal touch of her hand. The brand isn't printed on the work. It's left in it.

system.label: THE SYSTEM
system.text: One idea, carried from the name to the mark to the stamp in the clay. A brand that means one thing, everywhere — and that's what makes it impossible to mistake.
